title: Analytical toilet with acoustic transducers for collecting health-related measurement
abstract: The analytical toilet may include a seat, lid, and bowl as does a traditional toilet. It also includes one or more acoustic transducers. The acoustic transducers may be located on the seat where they may measure bone density in a users pelvis or femur. The acoustic transducers may be located on the lid or on a belt which wraps around the user. These acoustic transducers may collect data relating to a users heart, lungs, liver, bowel, or other internal organs. The analytical toilet may include a handrail with an acoustic transducer which measures the bone density in the users wrist, radius, or ulna. A controller associated with the analytical toilet may store algorithms for analyzing the data collected by the acoustic transducers. The controller may perform machine learning to improve the analyses and may calculate trends in repeated measurements taken from the same user or a population of users.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An analytical toilet comprising: a toilet seat; a toilet lid; a toilet bowl; at least one acoustic transducer disposed on one or more of the toilet seat, the toilet lid, or the toilet bowl; and a controller. 2. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein at least one acoustic transducer comprises a plurality of acoustic transducers. 3. The analytical toilet of Claim 2 wherein the plurality of acoustic transducers comprises a matrix array. 4. The analytical toilet of Claim 3 wherein the matrix array is disposed on the toilet lid. 5. The analytical toilet of Claim 3 wherein the controller comprises a non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for creating a two-dimensional image based on a series of acoustic signals detected by the plurality of acoustic transducers. 6. The analytical toilet of Claim 3 wherein the controller comprises a non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for creating a three-dimensional image based on a series of acoustic signals detected by the plurality of acoustic transducers. 7. The analytical toilet of Claim 3 wherein the controller comprises a non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for creating a four-dimensional image based on a series of acoustic signals detected by the plurality of acoustic transducers. 8. The analytical toilet of Claim 3 wherein the plurality of acoustic transducers is disposed on at least one sensor platform array. 9. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein at least one of the at least one acoustic transducers is disposed on the toilet seat. 10. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein at least one of the at least one acoustic transducers is disposed on the toilet lid. 11. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein at least one of the at least one acoustic transducers is directed towards the toilet bowl. 12. The analytical toilet of Claim 11 wherein the controller comprises non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for identifying one or more of the following list based on a series of acoustic signals detected by the at least one acoustic transducer: a length of urination time, urine volume, consistency of urine flow, flatulence, defecation, and volume of solid waste. 13. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 further comprising a belt, wherein at least one of the at least one acoustic transducers is disposed on an inner surface of the belt. 14. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein at least one of the at least one acoustic transducers emits Doppler signals. 15. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 further comprising an arm rest, wherein at least one of the at least one acoustic transducers is disposed on the arm rest. 16. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 further comprising a hand-held probe; wherein at least one of the at least one acoustic transducer is disposed on the hand-held probe. 17. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein the controller comprises non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for identifying a cardiomyopathy. 18. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein the controller comprises non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for determining bone density. 19. The analytical toilet of Claim 1 wherein the controller comprises non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for calculating trends in measurements collected by the at least one acoustic transducer. 20. The analytical toilet of Claim 19 wherein the controller comprises non-transitory computer readable medium which stores instructions for transmitting the trends in measurements to a remote device.
